response.overview: Miquelina, Josep and, later, their son Pattet filmed their lives between 1942 and 1999 with no purpose other than creating memories for the future. Without realizing it, they captured a face of the 20th century through the everyday life of a family close to the powers of the State, as a counterpoint to the country’s reality and the postwar period. A camera passed from hand to hand, a dictatorship transforming into a democracy, and images filled with aspirations, contradictions, and dreams of change.
response.title: We Were a Great Family
